reasoning: The community prediction is extremely low at 1.8%, and achieving a formal treaty obligation restricting solar geoengineering signed by at least 10 countries by the end of 2025 is highly unlikely. International treaties take years to negotiate, draft, and ratify. While there is an initiative for an International Non-Use Agreement, there is no major diplomatic momentum currently pointing toward 10 countries formally committing to such a treaty before 2026. Given the tight timeline and the current status of solar geoengineering governance (mostly academic and non-binding UN discussions rather than formal treaties), the probability is very low.
